operating_definitions.root: A NAMED, LEAST-COMMON-DENOMINATOR detection plot — the lowest-level named primitive humans refer to. The bible STOPS at this level. Internal mechanics (Supertrend, OKE engine, Zoo engine, EMA / SMA / WMA / VWMA, ATR multipliers, σ-multipliers, raw highest()/lowest() calls) are NOT roots.
operating_definitions.composite: A detection plot whose composition uses ≥1 root or lower-tier composite by name.
operating_definitions.tier: Depth from root. Root = 0. Composite-of-roots = 1. Composite-of-tier-1 = 2. ...

lifecycle_stage.2: Independent root calculation (decoupled, no cross-root ordering).
lifecycle_stage.5: Composite evaluation (reads post-offset boolean outputs only).
lifecycle_stage.7: Alert firing (always at bar close, regardless of plot offset).

operating_definitions.canonical_name: <provenance>::<name>. Provenance is the indicator study where this primitive is canonically defined.
non_roots: raw FVG geometry primitives (when not exposed as a named root)

cross_indicator_dependencies: PUP — locally redefined as sPPBull (line 59); also a known cross-indicator primitive (e.g. b2b-pup::PUP)
PPD — locally redefined as sPPBear (line 60); also a known cross-indicator primitive (e.g. hvd-pbj-ppd::PPD)
uncertain_or_questions: GapUp/GapDown are named conditionals (gapUpSig/gapDnSig) but have no standalone plotshape — only consumed by AllThreeBull/AllThreeBear. Listed as roots since they are named primitives, but caller may prefer to demote them to internal_helpers since they never emit independently.

AnishBull/AnishBear are pure EMA-stack composites (multi-EMA cross conditions plus 52w distance). The rule says 'EMA crosses are excluded from root catalog'; however these are NAMED stage-trend primitives (Stage 2 / Stage 4), so they were promoted to roots and the underlying EMA stack was kept as internal_helper. Confirm this interpretation.

TB and Foster reset behavior: counter resets only when reachedMin* is true; the 'else if not sPPBear and not bullPass' branch (line 99) only fires after threshold reached, which means the X-bar Anish run can be interrupted by neutral bars without resetting consecAnishBull (it resets only when bullPass becomes false then stays false — actually consecAnishBull resets every non-bullPass bar via line 88). Documented as-is.
Line count reported as 181 (last non-empty line is 180; file ends with trailing comment line 181).

roots.plain_english: Bullish push: bar closes ≥3% above its open AND its volume exceeds the highest red-bar volume in the prior 10 bars (red volume of the current bar excluded).
roots.boolean_summary: conf AND (close-open)/open*100 > pp_barSize AND volume > ta.highest(pp_redVol[1], pp_lookback)
roots.plot_call: (no direct plot — det_PUP feeds det_b2bPUP and S-plots; bar[0] anchor)
roots.notes: Definition is local. PUP is named here as a primitive (Anish identifies PUP as a root). Likely overlaps with hvd-pbj-ppd's PUP — bible may need to designate one canonical.

roots.plain_english: Bearish pocket-pivot day: bar closes ≥3% below its open AND its volume exceeds the highest green-bar volume in the prior 10 bars (green volume of the current bar excluded).
roots.boolean_summary: conf AND (open-close)/open*100 > pp_barSize AND volume > ta.highest(pp_greenVol[1], pp_lookback)
roots.plot_call: (no direct plot — det_PPD feeds det_b2bPPD and S-plots; bar[0] anchor)
roots.notes: Mirror of PUP for the down direction. Same parameter inputs.

roots.plain_english: FAUNA bull bar: an up-bar that satisfies at least one of MB (Momentum Bar: body ≥1.6×ATR14, body/range ≥0.70, vol ≥1.8×AvgVol), RE (Range Expansion: range ≥2.2×ATR14, close near high, vol ≥1.8×AvgVol), or TA (Trend Acceleration: rising 50-SMA, Δclose ≥1.6×AvgΔ, vol ≥1.8×AvgVol); the GG (Gap-and-Go) family is only allowed when 2+ core-FAUNA hit AND body/range ≥0.80; excluded by TR/ES/GDR (post-weak/strong/gap-down-reversal pre-bar context).
roots.boolean_summary: conf AND (f_MB_b OR f_RE_b OR f_TA_b) AND NOT (f_TR_b OR f_ES_b OR f_GDR_b OR (f_GG_b AND NOT (core>=2 AND bodyRat>=0.80)))
roots.plot_call: (no direct plot — det_FAUNABull is consumed by S2/S4/Matrix paths; bar[0] anchor)
roots.notes: Per v4.30 changelog the 7 family checkboxes were removed and all are hardcoded ON. Whether FAUNA is a 'root' or composite-of-named-primitives depends on the bible's stance on MB/RE/GG/TA being individually-named family primitives.

roots.plain_english: FAUNA bear bar: mirror of FAUNA_BULL — down-bar satisfying at least one of MB/RE/TA bear families, with GG-bear only allowed via the 2-core + bodyRat≥0.80 path; excluded by mirror TR/ES/GDR.
roots.boolean_summary: conf AND (f_MB_r OR f_RE_r OR f_TA_r) AND NOT (f_TR_r OR f_ES_r OR f_GDR_r OR (f_GG_r AND NOT (core>=2 AND bodyRat>=0.80)))
roots.plot_call: (no direct plot — det_FAUNABear is consumed by S2/S4/Matrix paths; bar[0] anchor)
roots.notes: Same as FAUNA_BULL questions about root vs composite status.

roots.plain_english: Bullish 3-bar FVG: prior bar's body (or range) exceeds disp_mult × stdev (default 5×) AND a bullish FVG forms (low > high[2] AND close[1] > open[1]).
roots.boolean_summary: conf AND disp_rng[1] > disp_thresh[1] AND low > high[2] AND close[1] > open[1]

parameters.name: disp_type
parameters.units: enum {Open to Close, High to Low}

roots.plot_call: (no direct plot — det_DISPBull feeds S3/S4/S6/UU paths; bar[1] anchor)
roots.notes: Anchored to the FVG-creation bar via offset=-1 in plotshapes that consume it.

roots.plain_english: Bearish 3-bar FVG: prior bar's body (or range) exceeds disp_mult × stdev AND a bearish FVG forms (high < low[2] AND close[1] < open[1]).
roots.boolean_summary: conf AND disp_rng[1] > disp_thresh[1] AND high < low[2] AND close[1] < open[1]
roots.plot_call: (no direct plot — det_DISPBear consumed by S3/S4/S6/UU; bar[1] anchor)
roots.notes: Mirror of DISPBull.

roots.plain_english: PBJ bull cross-firing: a Supertrend (over VWMA/EMA/SMA/WMA/HMA base) bullish crossover that lands at a previously-armed PBJ buy level (low < pbj_ma*(1-zoo_thresh), low = lowest of zoo_pbj_hh_ll, vol > avg_vol*zoo_pbj_vol_mult).
roots.boolean_summary: conf AND ta.crossover(close, sig_line) AND wait_pbj_buy
roots.plot_call: (no direct plot — det_PBJBull feeds S6/UU and HVD-PBJ chains; bar[0] anchor)
roots.notes: PBJ is the entry-quality refinement of the PB level-approach engine. det_PBBull (line 262) is the lower-tier 'PB but NOT PBJ' classifier — also a named primitive in this file.

roots.plain_english: Mirror PBJ bear: Supertrend bearish crossunder + previously-armed PBJ sell level (high > pbj_ma*(1+zoo_thresh), high = highest of zoo_pbj_hh_ll, vol > avg_vol*zoo_pbj_vol_mult).

Raw bull TNT: simultaneous (within tnt_CW=2*EMA_SLOW window) confluence of (a) volume-weighted EMA crossover zone, (b) Anish swing-pivot zone, (c) Flux/displacement zone, with strong-bull side test (median-vol gate, EMA slope > 0, RSI < 80, > 30% vertical zone overlap).

cross_indicator_dependencies.notes: Engine G (TNT/Napalm/Charge/CONT/TNT 2.0/Return-to-TNT) is required by README and CHANGELOG to mirror TNT OD's definitions exactly. Per v4.30 changelog the rewrite ported TNT OD's full architecture; v4.32 fixed a Charge-ladder regression detected by re-auditing against TNT OD.
PUP and PPD are defined locally here at L78-L79 with identical-looking semantics. The bible may need to designate one indicator's definition as canonical and mark the other as a duplicate.
SAAB/1×/GS/Pentagon/WTC/Hiroshima/Nagasaki/Kratos/MOAB tiers and the f_th_* threshold tables overlap in concept and naming with heavy-pentagon's pipelines P1/P2/P3.
CS1/CS2/CS3 (Unified) here mirror the combo families used by heavy-pentagon → heavy-combo-toggles, but the implementations are local.

uncertain_or_questions: Is PUP/PPD the canonical definition here, or is hvd-pbj-ppd canonical? Both files appear to define the same primitive — bible should pick one.

FAUNA: should this be modeled as a single root, or should MB / RE / GG / TA be the named roots and FAUNA be a tier-1 composite of them? The CHANGELOG and code treat the family-level name as primary (checkboxes were removed), suggesting FAUNA-level is the primitive.
PB-only (det_PBBull/Bear) vs PBJ (det_PBJBull/Bear): is PB a separately-named primitive in the bible, or just a PBJ near-miss?

All RVOL tiers (SAAB/Kratos/RVOL1x/GrandSlam/MOAB/Pentagon/WTC/Hiroshima/Nagasaki) are listed as roots since they are named primitives. They could also be modeled as a single 'RVOL ladder' composite of one underlying band-classification engine — bible should decide granularity.

Napalm vs Charge: per v4.32 CHANGELOG, Anish considers Napalm and Charge 'the same conceptual signal'. Should they be merged in the bible, or kept distinct (since their detection bodies are different)?

TNT 2.0 (super-TNT) and consolidated TNT_CONS: are these separately-named primitives or just internal scaffolding? They get folded into det_bullTNT/det_bearTNT before any plot, but they have distinct semantics.
S7 is intentionally missing from the S-numbered scheme (per README and v4.29 baseline).
Sibling file B2B_PUP_v4.32.pine is byte-for-byte equivalent except for line endings (Combined uses CRLF, sibling uses LF). They appear to be the same release packaged twice.

internal_helpers.name: dispBull / dispBear / noDisp
internal_helpers.purpose: Internal direction classifier; gates Heavy Combo composites into Bull/Bear/Neutral buckets
internal_helpers.notes: Computed from bar[1] range > σ × strength + bar[0] FVG confirmation. dispBull requires bar[1] bullish candle + range exceeds σ threshold + bar[0] bullish FVG. dispBear is the bearish mirror. noDisp = neither dispBull nor dispBear. Internal FVG detection (bFVG/sFVG) and σ range check (dispCandleMet) are sub-internals of this helper. NOT a root; NOT a composite. Used ONLY by Stage 5 Heavy Combo direction classification.

uncertain_or_questions: Heavy Combo composites use OR-of-roots inside their AND structure (e.g. baseYinYang = (sigBullRVOL1x or sigGrandSlam or sigBearRVOL1x or sigMOAB) and (sigPentagon or sigWTC or sigHiroshima)). The composition.type is recorded as AND because the top-level operator combining the two operand groups is AND, but the operand list flattens the inner ORs — every listed root is a potential contributor, not a required co-occurrence. Downstream consumers should treat operand lists as 'any-of within group, AND across groups' per the helper definitions in L278-L287.

internal_helpers.purpose: Boolean flag: prior bar's volume equals the highest volume over 5000 / 252 / 63 lookbacks (any of the three triggers HV).
internal_helpers.notes: Internal predicate consumed by HV root computation; HVE/HVY/HVQ are PARAMETERS of HV, not separate roots (L53-L63).

internal_helpers.name: gziProximity (input)
internal_helpers.purpose: Bar-distance threshold for GZI overlap (default 6 per CHANGELOG).
internal_helpers.notes: Parameter of GZI root, not a separate root (L21).

internal_helpers.name: fvg_records / fvg_areas
internal_helpers.purpose: Arrays of unmitigated FVG records and their drawn boxes; iterated for GZI overlap checks and mitigation processing.
internal_helpers.notes: State containers, not roots (L88-L89).

internal_helpers.name: bull_fvg_raw / bear_fvg_raw (raw chart-TF detection)
internal_helpers.purpose: Raw 3-bar gap detection on chart timeframe with auto/manual threshold; passed through request.security at the user-selected timeframe.
internal_helpers.notes: Producers of FVG_BULL_RAW / FVG_BEAR_RAW after MTF resolution at L73.

roots.plain_english: Fires on a newly detected bullish FVG when a same-polarity (bullish) FVG already exists within gziProximity bars AND the two zones overlap in price (or merely touch when both are HV).
roots.boolean_summary: bull_fvg AND fvg_time != lastTime AND showGZI AND any prior bullish fvg_record with (n - existing.barIdx) <= gziProximity AND (price-overlap OR adjacent-when-both-HV)
roots.plot_call: plotshape(bullGZI_trigger, title="Bullish GZI", style=shape.flag, location=location.top, color=gziBullCss, size=size.normal, offset=-1)
roots.notes: GZI overlap = price overlap (overlap_bottom < overlap_top) OR adjacent (overlap_bottom <= overlap_top) when both existing and new FVG are HV. Same polarity required (existing.isbull). Loop breaks on first match.

roots.plain_english: Fires on a newly detected bearish FVG when a same-polarity (bearish) FVG already exists within gziProximity bars AND the two zones overlap in price (or merely touch when both are HV).
roots.boolean_summary: bear_fvg AND fvg_time != lastTime AND showGZI AND any prior bearish fvg_record with (n - existing.barIdx) <= gziProximity AND (price-overlap OR adjacent-when-both-HV)
roots.plot_call: plotshape(bearGZI_trigger, title="Bearish GZI", style=shape.flag, location=location.top, color=gziBearCss, size=size.normal, offset=-1)
roots.notes: Mirror of GZI_BULL on bearish polarity (not existing.isbull). Adjacent-HV rule and break-on-first-match identical.

roots.plain_english: Fires on a newly detected bullish FVG when the prior bar's volume equals the highest volume over 5000, 252, or 63 bars (HVE / HVY / HVQ milestone).
roots.boolean_summary: bull_fvg AND fvg_time != lastTime AND showHV AND (volume[1] == highest(volume,5000)[1] OR volume[1] == highest(volume,252)[1] OR volume[1] == highest(volume,63)[1])
roots.plot_call: plotshape(bullHV_trigger, title="Bullish HV", style=shape.flag, location=location.belowbar, color=hvBullCss, size=size.normal, offset=-1)
roots.notes: HVE 5000 / HVY 252 / HVQ 63 lookbacks are PARAMETERS of HV root, not separate roots. Uses prior bar (volume[1]) so signal aligns with the FVG-formation bar (the middle bar of the 3-bar gap).

roots.plain_english: Fires on a newly detected bearish FVG when the prior bar's volume equals the highest volume over 5000, 252, or 63 bars (HVE / HVY / HVQ milestone).
roots.boolean_summary: bear_fvg AND fvg_time != lastTime AND showHV AND (volume[1] == highest(volume,5000)[1] OR volume[1] == highest(volume,252)[1] OR volume[1] == highest(volume,63)[1])
roots.plot_call: plotshape(bearHV_trigger, title="Bearish HV", style=shape.flag, location=location.belowbar, color=hvBearCss, size=size.normal, offset=-1)
roots.notes: Mirror of HV_BULL on bearish polarity. HVE/HVY/HVQ lookbacks are parameters, not separate roots.

roots.plain_english: Raw bullish 3-bar Fair Value Gap: current low above the high of two bars ago, with prior close confirming above that gap, and the gap size exceeding an auto (cumulative-avg-range) or manual percentage threshold. Resolved at the user-selected timeframe via request.security.
roots.boolean_summary: low > high[2] AND close[1] > high[2] AND (low - high[2]) / high[2] > threshold   [threshold = auto ? cum((high-low)/low)/bar_index : thresholdPer/100; resolved at tf via request.security; one-shot via fvg_time != lastTime]
roots.plot_call: (no dedicated plotshape; visualized as bullCss FVG box; alertcondition only)
roots.notes: FVG_BULL_RAW is the raw detection root; the GZI_BULL and HV_BULL plots are composites that depend on this root being true (plus their own predicates). Box draw extends n+480 bars (extend = 480, L32, L138). Alert fires when bull_count increments (L240).

roots.plain_english: Raw bearish 3-bar Fair Value Gap: current high below the low of two bars ago, with prior close confirming below that gap, and the gap size exceeding an auto (cumulative-avg-range) or manual percentage threshold. Resolved at the user-selected timeframe via request.security.
roots.boolean_summary: high < low[2] AND close[1] < low[2] AND (low[2] - high) / high > threshold   [threshold = auto ? cum((high-low)/low)/bar_index : thresholdPer/100; resolved at tf via request.security; one-shot via fvg_time != lastTime]
roots.plot_call: (no dedicated plotshape; visualized as bearCss FVG box; alertcondition only)
roots.notes: Mirror of FVG_BULL_RAW. Note bear_fvg_raw uses (low[2] - high) / high in the denominator (vs / high[2] for bull) — verbatim from L71. Box draw extends n+480 bars. Alert fires when bear_count increments (L241).

uncertain_or_questions: FVG_BULL_RAW / FVG_BEAR_RAW have no dedicated plotshape in this file — they are surfaced visually as boxes (bullCss/bearCss) and via the 'Bullish FVG' / 'Bearish FVG' alertconditions tied to bull_count / bear_count incrementing. Cataloged as roots because they are the named LCD detection that GZI/HV plots depend on, and because the suite-wide 'FVG bull/bear raw' canonicalization is hosted here. Confirm this matches the bible's root convention (named primitive even when not directly plotshape'd).

Plotshape count = 4 (Bull/Bear GZI top, Bull/Bear HV belowbar) — matches CHANGELOG. Alertcondition count = 10 (FVG bull/bear creation + bull/bear mitigation + GZI bull/bear/any + HV bull/bear/any) — matches CHANGELOG. Confirmed.
Per CHANGELOG, all plotshapes use offset=-1 — confirmed at L226, L229, L232, L235.
GZI 'adjacent-HV' rule (overlap_bottom <= overlap_top when both FVGs are HV) is part of the GZI root definition, not a separate root — treated as a parameter/branch of GZI_BULL/BEAR (L126, L166).

indicators.one_line_purpose: Canonical PBJ/PPD/PB/Displacement engine; consolidated 'THE_ONLY_ONE' build with four decoupled pipelines (A=HV+D, B=PBJ/PB, C=USE 45-signal alarm, D=Triple AND co-occurrence) plus a Back-to-Back HV+D section
is_canonical_for: HV ladder tiers exposed standalone (HV5K/HVY/HVQ inside GZI; HV75/HV150/HV500/HV1000 in USE engine)
indicators.predecessor_relationship: drifted-subset — current is a tightened consolidation: 1767 vs 1939 lines, 59 vs 58 plotshapes; 4.26 file is older fragmented variant. The 2026-05-10 build adds the formalized 4-pipeline architecture, the new B2B HV+D section (6 toggles) and the explicit Pipeline-D triple AND. CHANGELOG calls it the canonical build superseding the predecessor.

internal_helpers.name: Supertrend (Zoo ST)
internal_helpers.purpose: Trend direction (st_dir ±1) used inside Pipeline B (PBJ/PB engine) — `zoo_st_period=10`, `zoo_st_mult=2.0`.
internal_helpers.notes: Internal to PBJ/PB roots; NOT a separate root.

internal_helpers.name: Zoo MA (VWMA)
internal_helpers.purpose: Base MA for the PBJ/PB lander system (`zoo_ma_type=VWMA`, `zoo_ma_len=5`).
internal_helpers.notes: Internal to PBJ/PB roots; NOT a separate root.

internal_helpers.name: PB&J filter (pbj_buy / pbj_sell)
internal_helpers.purpose: ATR-pullback gate on lowest-low / highest-high over 25 bars + volume>SMA*0.1; gates `wait_pbj_*` flags consumed by buy/sell crosses.
internal_helpers.notes: Internal to PBJ root; do NOT decompose.

internal_helpers.name: PB lander (lvl array)
internal_helpers.purpose: Maintains an array of `lvl` boxes derived from buy/sell crosses + reaccel events; `f_check_approach` sets `wait_buy/sell` consumed by the PB cross.
internal_helpers.notes: Internal to PB root.

internal_helpers.name: ATR (atr14)
internal_helpers.purpose: Shared 14-period ATR for displacement, PB lander box sizing, and PBJ ATR threshold.
internal_helpers.notes: Internal helper.

internal_helpers.name: Stdev range (ta.stdev of |O-C| or H-L)
internal_helpers.purpose: Volatility yardstick used by all displacement primitives (D1/D2/D3 in Pipeline A and Engine 3 in Pipeline C).
internal_helpers.notes: Internal helper.

internal_helpers.name: BB normalized price/volume (bb_normalizedPrice, bb_normalizedVolume)
internal_helpers.purpose: Spike size and volume normalized vs 30-bar SMA, drives RVOL tier classification (SAAB/Kratos/1x/GS/MOAB/Pentagon).
internal_helpers.notes: Internal to RVOL tier classification (Pipeline C engine 1).

internal_helpers.name: TradingView/ta/7 relativeVolume (regular + cumulative)
internal_helpers.purpose: Imported library used to build relVolRatio (WTC/Hiroshima) and ls_regRatio/ls_cumRatio (Long/Short engine).
internal_helpers.notes: Internal helper.

internal_helpers.name: Boom Hunter Ehlers filter (HP/Filt/Quotient1..3, wt1/wt2)
internal_helpers.purpose: Multi-stage Ehlers band-pass + traditional CCI hybrid producing `bh_anyOmega` etc.
internal_helpers.notes: Internal to Omega Long composite — NOT a separate root.

internal_helpers.name: Ping Pong SR engine (srLevels array, swings, body-flat detect)
internal_helpers.purpose: Maintains support/resistance levels; emits `bull_pp` / `bear_pp` floor/ceiling state booleans.
internal_helpers.notes: Internal to Floor/Rooftop/2F/Penthouse composites.

internal_helpers.name: FAUNA NRA core (MB/RE/TA/GG patterns)
internal_helpers.purpose: ATR/body/range/volume hybrid emitting sigFAUNABull/Bear with TR/ES/GDR/GG exclusions.
internal_helpers.notes: Internal to many composites; not exposed as a standalone plot here.

roots.plain_english: Pipeline-A primitive: prior bar was a high-volume bar (one of the toggled rank tiers 50–1000 base, or HTF profile rank, or all-time HEV) AND prior bar's range exceeded stdev*mult AND a bullish FVG opened on the current bar. Fires on bar N to describe bar N-1; plotted with offset=-1.
roots.boolean_summary: hvd_fire_bull = base_combo_bull or htf1_combo_bull or htf2_combo_bull
roots.plot_call: plotshape(en_hvd_bull and hvd_fire_bull, "HV+D Bull", shape.triangleup,   location.belowbar, color.new(#00E676, 0), text="HV+D", textcolor=color.white, size=size.small, offset=-1)
roots.notes: Composite of three internal primitives (volume-rank, stdev displacement, FVG) but treated as a single root per definition. Internal: ta.highest(volume,N), ta.stdev range, FVG geometry. Plot offset=-1; alert at L1326 (excludes co-occurrence cases).

roots.plain_english: Bear mirror of HVD_BULL: prior bar high-volume rank ∧ prior bar disp ∧ bearish FVG. Pipeline A terminal output.
roots.boolean_summary: hvd_fire_bear = base_combo_bear or htf1_combo_bear or htf2_combo_bear
roots.plot_call: plotshape(en_hvd_bear and hvd_fire_bear, "HV+D Bear", shape.triangledown, location.abovebar, color.new(#FF1744, 0), text="HV+D", textcolor=color.white, size=size.small, offset=-1)
roots.notes: Same internals as HVD_BULL with reversed FVG/displacement direction. Alert at L1328.

roots.plain_english: Pipeline-B bullish PBJ landing: a Supertrend buy_cross (Zoo VWMA-5 vs ATR-band Supertrend) coincident with the PB&J filter (low<EMA20*(1-ATR/close*3) AND low==25-bar lowest AND volume>SMA20*0.1).
roots.boolean_summary: sigBullPBJ = conf and ta.crossover(close, st_signal_line) and wait_pbj_buy
roots.plot_call: (no standalone plot; consumed by composites + Pipeline D — see PBJ Bull at L1318 within HV+D co-occurrence at offset=-1, and SUPER/FLOOR/HW etc.)
roots.notes: Internal: VWMA + ATR-band Supertrend + PB&J filter. Do NOT decompose into Supertrend/Zoo/VWMA/ATR — those are internal helpers. Pipeline B fires bar N, describes bar N, offset=0. Note: there is no offset=0 standalone plot for sigBullPBJ in this file — it surfaces only via composites and Pipeline D's `hvd_pbj_bull`.

roots.plain_english: Bear mirror of PBJ_BULL: Supertrend sell_cross + PB&J sell filter (high>EMA20*(1+thresh), high==25-bar highest, volume>SMA20*0.1).
roots.boolean_summary: sigBearPBJ = conf and ta.crossunder(close, st_signal_line) and wait_pbj_sell
roots.plot_call: (no standalone plot; surfaces via composites and Pipeline D)
roots.notes: Mirror of PBJ_BULL with crossunder/highest/upper-band ATR threshold.

roots.plain_english: Pipeline-B bullish 'plain' Pullback (PB) landing: Supertrend buy_cross when price has approached a previously-recorded bullish lander box (from a prior cross or reaccel). Fires only when PBJ filter did NOT also fire.
roots.boolean_summary: sigBullPB = conf and enable_PB and (buy_cross and wait_buy) and not (buy_cross and wait_pbj_buy)
roots.plot_call: (no standalone plot; consumed via composites and Pipeline D — see HV+D+PB co-shape at L1317)
roots.notes: Internal: Zoo lander array `bull_lvls` of `lvl` (upper/lower/vol/approached) populated by f_add_lander on buy_cross and f_add_reaccel on bull_reaccel; f_check_approach sets wait_buy.

roots.plain_english: Bear mirror of PB_BULL: Supertrend sell_cross + bear lander box approached, excluding PBJ-sell coincidence.
roots.boolean_summary: sigBearPB = conf and enable_PB and (sell_cross and wait_sell) and not (sell_cross and wait_pbj_sell)
roots.plot_call: (no standalone plot; surfaces via composites and Pipeline D)
roots.notes: Mirror of PB_BULL on bear lvls.

roots.plain_english: Push-Up Day: bar's percent body (close-open)/open*100 > 3% AND volume > 10-bar highest red-bar volume. The classic 'green push' primitive.
roots.boolean_summary: sigPUP = conf and pp_priceUp and volume > pp_hiRedVol
roots.plot_call: (no standalone plot in this file; consumed by composites only — Golf, PAF, OD, SUPER, SDuper, alpha-strike etc.)
roots.notes: Engine 5 'PUP/PPD'. Defined locally; NOT plotted as its own shape but is a named root used by many composites.

roots.plain_english: Push-Down Day: bar's percent body (open-close)/open*100 > 3% AND volume > 10-bar highest green-bar volume. Bear mirror of PUP.
roots.boolean_summary: sigPPD = conf and pp_priceDn and volume > pp_hiGreenVol
roots.plot_call: (no standalone plot; consumed by composites only — Golf Bear, PAF Bear, OD Bear, SUPER/SD Bear etc.)
roots.notes: Engine 5; named root.

roots.plain_english: Pipeline-C displacement: prior bar range > stdev*3.0 (capped *100), AND a bullish FVG opened on current bar (or current-bar disp+green if i_req_fvg=false).
roots.boolean_summary: sigDISPBull = conf and (i_req_fvg ? (disp_prevDisp and disp_bullFVG) : (disp_currDisp and close>open))
roots.plot_call: (no standalone plot; named root that gates many composites)
roots.notes: Engine 3 USE Displacement. Distinct from Pipeline-A HVD's displacement (which uses different multipliers and FVG semantics).

roots.plain_english: Bear mirror of DISP_BULL.
roots.boolean_summary: sigDISPBear = conf and (i_req_fvg ? (disp_prevDisp and disp_bearFVG) : (disp_currDisp and close<open))
roots.notes: Engine 3 mirror.

roots.plain_english: 5x-stdev displacement primitive (close>open ∧ range>stdev*5.0). Used inside HW Bull composite.
roots.boolean_summary: disp5_bull = conf and disp_std>0 and disp_rng>disp_std*5.0 and close>open
roots.notes: Hardcoded 5x; named primitive used by HW + UU exception.

roots.plain_english: Bear mirror of DISP5_BULL.
roots.boolean_summary: disp5_bear = conf and disp_std>0 and disp_rng>disp_std*5.0 and close<open
roots.notes: Mirror.

roots.plain_english: Volume on prior bar equals 5000-bar highest. The 'all-time' tier of the GZI HV ladder.
roots.boolean_summary: gz_v1 == ta.highest(volume,5000)[1]
roots.plot_call: (no standalone plot; rolled into gz_isHV and gz_bullHV/gz_bearHV)
roots.notes: Named tier of HV ladder used inside GZ1/HV-FVG (Engine 4).

roots.plain_english: Yearly tier — volume on prior bar equals 252-bar highest.
roots.boolean_summary: gz_v1 == ta.highest(volume,252)[1]
roots.notes: Named tier.

roots.plain_english: Quarterly tier — volume on prior bar equals 63-bar highest.
roots.boolean_summary: gz_v1 == ta.highest(volume,63)[1]
roots.notes: Named tier.

roots.plain_english: Volume >= 75-bar highest of prior bar. Named USE-side HV tier.
roots.boolean_summary: sigHV75 = conf and volume >= ta.highest(volume,75)[1]
roots.notes: Used by TF gate count and as a named root primitive.

roots.plain_english: Volume >= 150-bar (configurable hv150_len) highest of prior bar.
roots.boolean_summary: sigHV150 = conf and volume >= ta.highest(volume,hv150_len)[1]
roots.notes: Used by TF gate.

roots.plain_english: Volume >= 500-bar highest of prior bar.
roots.boolean_summary: sigHV500 = conf and volume >= ta.highest(volume,500)[1]
roots.notes: Used by Omega Long cosignal + TF gate.

roots.plain_english: Volume >= 1000-bar highest of prior bar.
roots.boolean_summary: sigHV1000 = conf and volume >= ta.highest(volume,1000)[1]
roots.notes: Used by Omega Long cosignal + TF gate.

roots.plain_english: All-time-high volume primitive (volume[1] strictly greater than running maxVolEver). Cumulative monotonic ratchet.
roots.boolean_summary: isHEV when volume[1] > maxVolEver (then maxVolEver := volume[1])
roots.notes: Named primitive driving Pipeline-A base_hv_hit. There is also a separate sigNagasaki primitive on USE side using a similar all-time-high construction (L465-L472).

roots.plain_english: USE-side all-time-high volume primitive (volume strictly greater than running maxVol since bar 0).
roots.boolean_summary: sigNagasaki = isNagasaki when volume>maxVol
roots.plot_call: (no standalone plot; used as cosignal across many composites; alerted in aggregator at L1765-L1767)
roots.notes: Distinct from HEV (uses bar-zero seeding rather than first-rise seeding). Family: RVOL/HV hybrid.

operands: (RVOL_1x or GS or WTC or HIRO or NAG)
((csNew1_Bull or csNew2_Bull) and PUP) or (FLOOR_BULL or SECOND_FLOOR_BULL)

composites.plain_english: Bear mirror of SUPER_BULL.

operands: (RVOL_1x_bear or MOAB or WTC or HIRO or NAG)
((csNew1_Bear or csNew2_Bear) and PPD) or (ROOFTOP or PENTHOUSE)

composites.plain_english: 'Super Duper' priority: Floor/2F + PBJ + HW-volume slot + Combo-set + PUP + DISP + (FAUNA or LSLong1).

operands: (RVOL_1x or GS or WTC or HIRO or NAG)

composites.plain_english: Bear mirror of SDUPER_BULL.

operands: (RVOL_1x_bear or MOAB or WTC or HIRO or NAG)

composites.plain_english: 4 consecutive bullish bars + must include first bar of trading day + PBJ on at least one of the 4 bars + DISP on at least one of the 4 bars.
composites.notes: u4_bull_streak ≥ 4 over 4-bar window with day-1 + PBJ + DISP scan.

composites.plain_english: Bear mirror of BULL_UUUU.
3 consecutive bullish bars each with bb_normalizedPrice>1.0 + first-of-day in window + PBJ on any + DISP on any.
Bear mirror of BULL_UUU.
2 consecutive bullish bars each ≥ normPrice 2.0 + cumulative ≥ RVOL 1x threshold + ((PBJ ∧ DISP on either) OR session-start excA OR PUP×2 excC).
composites.notes: Three exception paths (A/B/C) gate the simpler PBJ∧DISP path.

composites.plain_english: Bear mirror of BULL_UU.
3+ consecutive bullish bars each ≥ normPrice 2.0 + cum ≥ th_1x + PBJ on any + DISP on any.
Bear mirror of BULL_USUB.
First USE-signal of the day + Ping-Pong bull state + (GS or RVOL1x) + (PBJ or PB) + expanded FAUNA slot.
composites.notes: as_fauna_bull = FAUNA bull OR LSLong1 OR DISP_BULL OR PUP OR WTC OR Hiro OR Nag.

composites.plain_english: Bear mirror of ALPHA_STRIKE_BULL.
4 consecutive FAUNA-confirmed bullish bars.
Bear mirror — 4 consecutive FAUNA-confirmed bearish bars.
Boom Hunter omega trigger + any of ~35 bull cosignals + NOT MOAB + NOT bearish DISP.
composites.notes: Boom Hunter Ehlers internals; cosignal OR-bag of ~35 named bull primitives/composites.

composites.plain_english: Opening Drive Bull: within first od_max_bars of session + any FVG combo set + DISP prevDisp + PUP + PBJ_BULL.
Bear mirror of OD_BULL.
2+ consecutive DISP2 bars (range > stdev*3) AND FAUNA bull on current and prior.
composites.notes: DISP2 has its own group inputs (i_disp2_*).

composites.plain_english: Bear mirror — 2+ consecutive DISP2 bear bars + FAUNA bear x2.
3+ consecutive DISP3 bull bars + FAUNA bull on current, [1], and [2].
Bear mirror — 3+ consecutive DISP3 bear bars + FAUNA bear x3.
3-bar confirmed bull sequence: DISP_BULL now + FAUNA[1] + PUP[1] + DISP_BULL[1] + FAUNA[2] + PUP[2].
Bear mirror — DISP_BEAR + FAUNA bear[1] + PPD[1] + DISP_BEAR[1] + FAUNA bear[2] + PPD[2].
Back-to-back PUP + FAUNA bull (PUP now + FAUNA now + PUP[1] + FAUNA[1]).
composites.notes: Inline 'k' toggled label says PAF PUP B2B in inputs.

composites.plain_english: Bear mirror — PPD + FAUNA bear, with [1] also true.
FVG Combo (consolidated): body% ≥ 0.85 on prior bar + (gz_bullHV OR gz_bullGZI) + (SAAB or RVOL1x or GS) on prior.
composites.pine_source_line_range: L1037-L1050 (csNew1_Bull = comboSet1_Bull or comboSet2_Bull), plot L1353
composites.notes: csNew1_Bull is the consolidated FVG combo (RVOL slot OR HW slot). Plot offset=-1.

composites.plain_english: Bear mirror of CS1_FVG_BULL.
Matrix Combo (consolidated): body% ≥ 0.85 + matrix_any_bull (Neo/Trinity/aligned) + (SAAB|RVOL1x|GS or Pentagon|WTC|Hiro|Nag).
Bear mirror of CS2_MAT_BULL.
Unified Combo Bull: CS1 (FVG) AND CS2 (MAT) both present.
Bear mirror — CS1 ∧ CS2 both bear.
Combo Chain Bull: ≥ cc_min_hits Combo windows in last cc_window bars + a PBJ_BULL or PB_BULL within the window. Latches active until any/all combo expire.

Bear mirror of LSC_BULL.
Floor: Ping-Pong bull state + PBJ_BULL + (RVOL1x or GS or WTC or Hiro or directional Nag).

Penthouse: Ping-Pong bear + PB_BEAR + HW-volume slot bear.
HW Bull: green bar + DISP5 (5σ) + PBJ_BULL + HW-volume slot + (Floor or 2F).

operands: (GS or WTC or Hiro or directional Nag)

composites.plain_english: HW Bear: red bar + DISP5 bear + PBJ_BEAR + HW-volume slot bear + (Rooftop or Penthouse).

operands: (MOAB or WTC or Hiro or directional Nag)

composites.plain_english: HV+D bull AND PB_BULL on prior bar (offset alignment for the same described candle).
composites.plot_call: plotshape(en_hvd_pb_bull  and hvd_pb_bull,  "PB Bull",  shape.labelup,  location.bottom, color.new(color.aqua, 75),   size=size.small,  text="HV+D\nPB",  textcolor=color.white, offset=-1)

composites.plain_english: HV+D bull AND PBJ_BULL on prior bar.
composites.plot_call: plotshape(en_hvd_pbj_bull and hvd_pbj_bull, "PBJ Bull", shape.diamond,  location.bottom, color.new(color.yellow, 25), size=size.normal, text="HV+D\nPBJ", textcolor=color.white, offset=-1)

composites.plain_english: HV+D bear AND PB_BEAR on prior bar.
composites.plot_call: plotshape(en_hvd_pb_bear  and hvd_pb_bear,  "PB Bear",  shape.labeldown, location.top,   color.new(color.orange, 75), size=size.small,  text="HV+D\nPB",  textcolor=color.white, offset=-1)

If the bible expects a Pipeline-B standalone plot, it would have to be classified 'no plot — terminal-boolean root only'.

PUP and PPD also have no standalone plot here — they are named primitives consumed only by composites. Listed as roots per the family-instructions ('PUP if defined here'). Confirm whether bible wants them plotted-only or any-named-primitive.

Volume rank tiers (50,75,100,...,1000) inside Pipeline A are NOT individually exposed as separate roots — they roll up into base_combo_bull via the priority ranker. Listed only HV5K/HVY/HVQ (GZI ladder) and HV75/HV150/HV500/HV1000 (USE-side ladder) as named primitives because those names are explicitly emitted as Boolean variables. The Pipeline-A 50/100/150/.../1000 toggles are all internal to the single HVD root.

DISP2 / DISP3 are intermediate primitives used only inside DISP_CONS_BULL2/3; not separately rooted. DISP5 is exposed because HW Bull/Bear depend on it (and uses a hardcoded 5σ which differs from DISP's user-input multipliers).

Several composite operands listed as bare Pine identifiers (e.g. sigFAUNABull, bull_pp, gz_bullGZI, sigSAAB, sigGrandSlam, sigPentagon, sigWTC, sigHiroshima, sigMOAB, sigKratos, sigBullRVOL1x, sigBearRVOL1x, sigLong1, sigShort1, sigNeoBull, sigTrinityBull) refer to internal USE-engine primitives. internal_helpers.notes: Not a root; internal counter state used by alerts (L81, L85, L197, L204, L242-L243). Identical to canonical.

internal_helpers.name: bull_count / bear_count
internal_helpers.purpose: Counters incremented on each new FVG; referenced by 'Bullish FVG' / 'Bearish FVG' alertconditions (FVG creation alerts).
internal_helpers.notes: Not a root; counter state used by FVG-creation alerts (L80, L84, L140, L180, L240-L241). Identical to canonical.

internal_helpers.name: isFormationHV / isFormationHVE / isFormationHVY / isFormationHVQ

internal_helpers.notes: State containers, not roots (L88-L89). Identical to canonical.

internal_helpers.name: bull_fvg_raw / bear_fvg_raw (raw chart-TF detection)
internal_helpers.purpose: Raw 3-bar gap detection on chart timeframe with auto/manual threshold; passed through request.security at the user-selected timeframe.

roots.plain_english: Fires on a newly detected bullish FVG when a same-polarity (bullish) FVG already exists within gziProximity bars AND the two zones overlap in price (or merely touch when both are HV).
roots.boolean_summary: bull_fvg AND fvg_time != lastTime AND showGZI AND any prior bullish fvg_record with (n - existing.barIdx) <= gziProximity AND (price-overlap OR adjacent-when-both-HV)
roots.plot_call: plotshape(bullGZI_trigger, title="Bullish GZI", style=shape.flag, location=location.top, color=gziBullCss, size=size.normal, offset=-1)
roots.notes: Logic verbatim from canonical hv-fvg-gz1-og::GZI_BULL — same overlap rule (price-overlap OR adjacent-HV), same break-on-first-match, same parameters. Stage 1.5 byte-diff vs canonical recommended.

roots.plain_english: Fires on a newly detected bearish FVG when a same-polarity (bearish) FVG already exists within gziProximity bars AND the two zones overlap in price (or merely touch when both are HV).
roots.boolean_summary: bear_fvg AND fvg_time != lastTime AND showGZI AND any prior bearish fvg_record with (n - existing.barIdx) <= gziProximity AND (price-overlap OR adjacent-when-both-HV)
roots.plot_call: plotshape(bearGZI_trigger, title="Bearish GZI", style=shape.flag, location=location.top, color=gziBearCss, size=size.normal, offset=-1)
roots.notes: Mirror of GZI_BULL on bearish polarity (not existing.isbull). Adjacent-HV rule and break-on-first-match identical to canonical. Stage 1.5 byte-diff vs canonical recommended.

roots.plain_english: Fires on a newly detected bullish FVG when the prior bar's volume equals the highest volume over 5000, 252, or 63 bars (HVE / HVY / HVQ milestone).
roots.boolean_summary: bull_fvg AND fvg_time != lastTime AND showHV AND (volume[1] == highest(volume,5000)[1] OR volume[1] == highest(volume,252)[1] OR volume[1] == highest(volume,63)[1])
roots.plot_call: plotshape(bullHV_trigger, title="Bullish HV", style=shape.flag, location=location.belowbar, color=hvBullCss, size=size.normal, offset=-1)
roots.notes: HVE 5000 / HVY 252 / HVQ 63 lookbacks are PARAMETERS, not separate roots. Uses prior bar (volume[1]). Identical lookbacks and predicate to canonical. Stage 1.5 byte-diff vs canonical recommended.

roots.plain_english: Fires on a newly detected bearish FVG when the prior bar's volume equals the highest volume over 5000, 252, or 63 bars (HVE / HVY / HVQ milestone).
roots.boolean_summary: bear_fvg AND fvg_time != lastTime AND showHV AND (volume[1] == highest(volume,5000)[1] OR volume[1] == highest(volume,252)[1] OR volume[1] == highest(volume,63)[1])
roots.plot_call: plotshape(bearHV_trigger, title="Bearish HV", style=shape.flag, location=location.belowbar, color=hvBearCss, size=size.normal, offset=-1)
roots.notes: Mirror of HV_BULL on bearish polarity. HVE/HVY/HVQ lookbacks are parameters, not separate roots. Stage 1.5 byte-diff vs canonical recommended.

roots.plain_english: Raw bullish 3-bar Fair Value Gap: current low above the high of two bars ago, with prior close confirming above that gap, and the gap size exceeding an auto (cumulative-avg-range) or manual percentage threshold. Resolved at the user-selected timeframe via request.security.
roots.boolean_summary: low > high[2] AND close[1] > high[2] AND (low - high[2]) / high[2] > threshold   [threshold = auto ? cum((high-low)/low)/bar_index : thresholdPer/100; resolved at tf via request.security; one-shot via fvg_time != lastTime]
roots.plot_call: (no dedicated plotshape; visualized as bullCss FVG box; alertcondition only)
roots.notes: Same raw 3-bar gap predicate as canonical. Box draw extends n+480 (extend=480 hardcoded at L32). Alert fires when bull_count increments (L240). Stage 1.5 byte-diff vs canonical recommended.

roots.plain_english: Raw bearish 3-bar Fair Value Gap: current high below the low of two bars ago, with prior close confirming below that gap, and the gap size exceeding an auto (cumulative-avg-range) or manual percentage threshold. Resolved at the user-selected timeframe via request.security.
roots.boolean_summary: high < low[2] AND close[1] < low[2] AND (low[2] - high) / high > threshold   [threshold = auto ? cum((high-low)/low)/bar_index : thresholdPer/100; resolved at tf via request.security; one-shot via fvg_time != lastTime]
roots.plot_call: (no dedicated plotshape; visualized as bearCss FVG box; alertcondition only)
roots.notes: Mirror of FVG_BULL_RAW. Note bear_fvg_raw denominator is /high (vs /high[2] for bull) — verbatim from L71, identical to canonical. Box draw extends n+480 bars. Alert fires when bear_count increments (L241). Stage 1.5 byte-diff vs canonical recommended.

uncertain_or_questions: Trinity vs Neo (RESOLVED): The Squarify file defines BOTH inline at L1215-L1216. Neo bull = is_matrix_number AND sigFAUNABull. Trinity bull = is_matrix_number AND NOT sigFAUNABull AND close>open (i.e., a matrix-number bar that lacks FAUNA but is still directionally up). Neither is plotted directly as a numbered Squarify composite — they feed matrix_any_bull, which is consumed by Combo Sets 3/4 and indirectly by UC (csNew3). Their canonical home is squarify (not heavy-pentagon or fauna) since Squarify owns the Neo/Matrix-Number lookback parameter (neo_len=67).

Unified Combo (UC) (RESOLVED): UC = csNew3 = csNew1 (FVG combo set on bar[0]) AND csNew2[1] (matrix combo set on bar[1]). Defined at L1240-L1257. Used as a building block in S1, S2, S22, S27, S31, S32, S40, S44, S45, S46. Tagged as a local root because the composition logic is original to Squarify.

Several engines (RVOL, FAUNA, PBJ, GZI/HV-FVG, PUP, TNT, Boom Hunter, Long/Short, Heavy Pentagon Combos, Ping-Pong) are duplicated inline rather than imported. Their roots are emitted under the same canonical names as the source-of-truth indicators (heavy-pentagon, fauna, hvd-pbj-ppd, hv-fvg-gz1-og, b2b-pup, tnt-od, boom-hunter, long-short). Tagged provenance_uncertain only on B2B_PUP (the cleanest case where Squarify's local definition exactly matches the b2b-pup canonical definition).

Bear-side numbered plots: only S42 (WBUSH Bear) and S46 (UC NAGASAKI Bear) are wired to plotshapes. The aggregator computes most fire_*Bear booleans (DispConsBear2/3, GolfBear, PAFBear, FloorBear/Pent, HWBear, SuperBear, etc.) but they do NOT have plotshape calls in v2 — the bear pipeline is largely shows_*=false suppressed for plotting purposes, even though the engine logic exists. Confirmed by grepping plotshape calls.
S38 (FLR+UU) lives in the 'fusion' layer per architecture but uses zero offset rather than -1, since neither operand has displacement+FVG in the chain.
T1/T2 OPENING CONFLU plots are recorded as both roots and composites (the root is the boolean primitive defined uniquely here, the composite entry is the ALIAS plotshape).

roots.description: Event-log path: ≥2 same-direction events (TNT/Napalm/effective-Charge) accumulated, gated by MIN_SIG_GAP. Internally folded into det_bullTNT/det_bearTNT.

Three-clause sudden-change/proximity logic — Charge after Return within SUDDEN_PROX bars OR (TNT/TNT2 within SUDDEN_PROX of last Charge) OR Charge within SUDDEN_PROX of last TNT/TNT2.

internal_helpers.name: anyBullRVOL / anyBearRVOL
internal_helpers.purpose: RVOL-family aggregate explicitly excluding SAAB/Kratos, used inside Super-PBJ/Super-PB constructions (L750-L751)

internal_helpers.name: anyBullHW / anyBearHW
internal_helpers.purpose: Wider HW aggregate (RVOL family + sigROC + Super combos) used for HW combo plots (L870-L871)

internal_helpers.name: fauna_* (atr/avgVol/avgBody/avgRange/avgDelta/trendMA/body/rng2/bodySz/bodyRat/up/dn/prev_body/prev_range/StrongBear/WeakBear/StrongBull/WeakBull)
internal_helpers.purpose: FAUNA-scoped recomputation of the same MB/RE/TA arithmetic in independent variables, plus the strong/weak-bear/bull qualifiers used by ES/TR (L703-L745)

internal_helpers.name: fvgs (typed array) + lastT
internal_helpers.purpose: Rolling FVG bank consumed by GZI/HV detection. Bull/bear FVG defined L776-L777, bank evolves L787-L823

internal_helpers.name: gzThresh
internal_helpers.purpose: Cumulative-mean (high-low)/low scaled by bar_index — gating threshold for both bullish/bearish FVG widths (L775)

internal_helpers.name: f_hadSignalYesterday
internal_helpers.purpose: Lookback for any sig in the prior ~2 trading days (uses barsPerDay) to power the *_B2BDays_* combos and superB2BDays (L853-L859)

internal_helpers.name: f_hwStr / f_fullScan
internal_helpers.purpose: Alert message string builders (L925-L1013)

internal_helpers.name: scanTrigger
internal_helpers.purpose: Composite alert-only trigger = sigSuperBullPBJ or sigSuperBullPB or sigSuperBearPBJ or sigSuperBearPB or sigGrandSlam or sigMOAB (L920) — alertcondition-exposed but not plotted as a named shape

roots.plain_english: Bullish F2: at session bar 2, this and previous bar are both MB (momentum-body) bullish events
roots.boolean_summary: sBullF2 = conf and sessBar==2 and bull_MB and bull_MB[1]
roots.plot_call: (no dedicated plotshape; surfaced via f_fullScan as 'F2+' and via combo plots e.g. PBJ+F2)
roots.notes: F2 has no own plotshape but is named in alerts (F2+) and is the named atom that combos consume.

roots.plain_english: Bearish F2: at session bar 2, this and previous bar are both bearish MB events
roots.boolean_summary: sBearF2 = conf and sessBar==2 and bear_MB and bear_MB[1]
parameters: same MB thresholds as F2_BULL but with bodyDn
roots.plot_call: (no dedicated plotshape; surfaced as 'F2-' in scan + combo plots)

roots.plain_english: Bullish E3: at session bar 3, current and previous two bars each fired MB OR RE OR TA bull
roots.boolean_summary: sBullE3 = conf and sessBar==3 and b2_ev and b2_ev[1] and b2_ev[2] where b2_ev = bull_MB or bull_RE or bull_TA
roots.alertcondition_id: (component of PBJ+E3, F2CL→E3, E3⅔PP, E3×2D, F2E3seq)

roots.plain_english: Bearish E3: at session bar 3, current and previous two bars each fired bear MB/RE/TA
roots.boolean_summary: sBearE3 = conf and sessBar==3 and b5_ev and b5_ev[1] and b5_ev[2] where b5_ev = bear_MB or bear_RE or bear_TA

roots.plain_english: Bullish FC cluster: 2-of-3 cluster indicators true (consecutive MB / cumulative-day MB+RE+TA / consecutive in-session MB+RE) AND threshold-bar overlaps a sequence-bar within 20 bars
roots.boolean_summary: sBullFC = conf and b1_2of3 and b1_ovlp
parameters: rvol price spike vs 30-bar SMA prior (b1_rvolP)
roots.plot_call: (no dedicated plotshape; 'FC+' in scan + combo plots)
roots.notes: Engine spans L124-L206 with state arrays b1_thIdx/b1_sqIdx etc.

roots.plain_english: Bearish FC cluster mirror of FC_BULL
roots.boolean_summary: sBearFC = conf and b4_2of3 and b4_ovlp
parameters: mirror of bullish; sequence cumulative threshold = 0.5 (vs bull 0.1)
roots.notes: Bull cluster uses sum >= 0.1; bear uses sum >= 0.5 (intentional asymmetry — see L167 vs L254).

roots.plain_english: FAUNA Momentum-Body bull: confirmed bullish bar with body > 1.6*ATR, body/range > 0.7, volume > 1.8*avg20
roots.boolean_summary: fMB_b = conf and fauna_up and fauna_bodySz > 1.6*fauna_atr and fauna_bodyRat > 0.7 and volume > 1.8*fauna_avgVol
roots.plot_call: (no dedicated plotshape — internal to FAUNA composition)
roots.notes: First definition that locks the FAUNA-MB primitive.

roots.plain_english: FAUNA Momentum-Body bear (mirror of MB_BULL)
roots.boolean_summary: fMB_r = conf and fauna_dn and fauna_bodySz > 1.6*fauna_atr and fauna_bodyRat > 0.7 and volume > 1.8*fauna_avgVol

roots.plain_english: FAUNA Range-Expansion bull: confirmed bull bar with range > 2.2*ATR, close in top 15% of range, vol > 1.8*avg
roots.boolean_summary: fRE_b = conf and fauna_up and fauna_rng2 > 2.2*fauna_atr and (high-close) < 0.15*fauna_rng2 and volume > 1.8*fauna_avgVol

roots.plain_english: FAUNA Range-Expansion bear (mirror)
roots.boolean_summary: fRE_r = conf and fauna_dn and fauna_rng2 > 2.2*fauna_atr and (close-low) < 0.15*fauna_rng2 and volume > 1.8*fauna_avgVol

roots.plain_english: FAUNA Trend-Acceleration bull: 50-SMA rising, |close-close[1]| > 1.6*avg|delta|10, body up, vol > 1.8*avg
roots.boolean_summary: fTA_b = conf and fauna_trendMA > fauna_trendMA[1] and (close-close[1]) > 1.6*fauna_avgDelta and fauna_up and volume > 1.8*fauna_avgVol

roots.plain_english: FAUNA Trend-Acceleration bear (mirror)
roots.boolean_summary: fTA_r = conf and fauna_trendMA < fauna_trendMA[1] and (close[1]-close) > 1.6*fauna_avgDelta and fauna_dn and volume > 1.8*fauna_avgVol

roots.plain_english: FAUNA Gap-Gap bull exclusion atom: open gaps up > 0.9*ATR over prior close, body up, low > prior close, volume burst — used only to suppress FAUNA
roots.boolean_summary: fGG_b = (open-close[1]) > 0.9*fauna_atr and fauna_up and low > close[1] and volume > 1.8*fauna_avgVol
roots.plot_call: (internal — pure exclusion atom, never plotted standalone)
roots.notes: Note: GG_BULL deliberately omits 'conf' because it is consumed by FAUNA which is itself confirmed via the MB/RE/TA core.

roots.plain_english: FAUNA Gap-Gap bear exclusion atom
roots.boolean_summary: fGG_r = (close[1]-open) > 0.9*fauna_atr and fauna_dn and high < close[1] and volume > 1.8*fauna_avgVol

roots.plain_english: FAUNA TR (Trap-Reverse from weak-bear) bull exclusion: prior bar weak-bear (body/range <= 0.2) AND core MB/RE/TA fired
roots.boolean_summary: fTR_b = fauna_WeakBear and (fMB_b or fRE_b or fTA_b)
roots.notes: TR is a COMPOSITE in the strict sense (it ANDs MB/RE/TA on top of WeakBear), but the prompt calls it out as a primitive name; classified as root because it is referenced by NAME inside the FAUNA expression and has its own own boolean.

roots.plain_english: FAUNA TR bear exclusion (mirror)
roots.boolean_summary: fTR_r = fauna_WeakBull and (fMB_r or fRE_r or fTA_r)

roots.plain_english: FAUNA ES (Engulf-from-Strong-bear) bull exclusion: prior bar strong-bear (|body|>1.5*avgBody and vol>1.5*avgVol) AND core MB/RE/TA fires
roots.boolean_summary: fES_b = fauna_StrongBear and (fMB_b or fRE_b or fTA_b)
parameters: StrongBear: prior body factor 1.5, prior vol factor 1.5
roots.notes: Same root-classification rationale as TR.

roots.plain_english: FAUNA ES bear exclusion (mirror)
roots.boolean_summary: fES_r = fauna_StrongBull and (fMB_r or fRE_r or fTA_r)

roots.plain_english: FAUNA GDR (Gap Down + Reverse) bull exclusion: prior bar bearish AND GG_BULL fires
roots.boolean_summary: fGDR_b = close[1] < open[1] and fGG_b

roots.plain_english: FAUNA GDR bear exclusion (mirror)
roots.boolean_summary: fGDR_r = close[1] > open[1] and fGG_r

roots.plain_english: Atomic ROC-bull crossover primitive: ta.crossover(roc_ema, 2.0). Pre-merge with WaveTrend signals into bull2_cond.
roots.boolean_summary: b1_roc = ta.crossover(roc_ema, 2.0) (one of five ROC/LBWT atoms b1..b5_roc)
roots.notes: Five-atom set b1_roc..b5_roc and r1_roc..r5_roc (L661-L671). Defined here in ultra-combo namespace.

roots.plain_english: Atomic ROC-bear crossunder primitive: ta.crossunder(roc_ema, -1.25)
roots.boolean_summary: r1_roc = ta.crossunder(roc_ema, -1.25)

roots.plain_english: LazyBear WaveTrend bull cross: ld_xup = wt1 crosses over wt2 (with HLC3-EMA8/CI/EMA21/SMA4 stack). One of two raw cross atoms; combined with thresholds in b2..b5_roc.
roots.boolean_summary: ld_xup = ta.crossover(ld_wt1, ld_wt2)

roots.plain_english: LazyBear WaveTrend bear cross: ld_wt1 crosses under ld_wt2
roots.boolean_summary: ld_xdn = ta.crossunder(ld_wt1, ld_wt2)

roots.plain_english: TB (Trend Break-down): after 5+ consecutive bullPass days, on the first bar where bullPass turns false, open a 2-bar window; fire if a sPPBear (PUP-style bear) hits inside the window
roots.boolean_summary: tbSignal = (window armed by 5+ bullPass) and sPPBear inside 2-bar window
roots.plot_call: (no dedicated plotshape; surfaced via 'TB' in scan and via tbHeavyBear combo)
roots.notes: Variants tbPBJSignal / tbPBSignal fire if sigBearPBJ / sigBearPB enters window instead. Anish-style trap signals.

roots.plain_english: Foster (Trend Reversal-up): after 5+ consecutive bearPass days, on the first non-bearPass bar, open 2-bar window; fire if sPPBull (PUP-style bull) hits
roots.boolean_summary: fosterSignal = (window armed by 5+ bearPass) and sPPBull inside 2-bar window
roots.plot_call: (no dedicated plotshape; 'FOSTER' in scan, drives fosterHeavyBull combo)
roots.notes: fosterPBJSignal / fosterPBSignal mirror tbPBJSignal/tbPBSignal.

fauna_decomposition.expression: (MB OR RE OR TA) AND NOT (GG OR TR OR ES OR GDR)

exclusion_components.name: TR
exclusion_components.source_line: L727 (bull) / L743 (bear) — formula = WeakBear AND (MB OR RE OR TA); has its own boolean and is referenced by name, so classified as root per prompt

exclusion_components.name: ES
exclusion_components.source_line: L728 (bull) / L744 (bear) — formula = StrongBear AND (MB OR RE OR TA); same root rationale

exclusion_components.source_line: L729 (bull) / L745 (bear) — formula = (close[1]<open[1]) AND GG; same rationale

uncertain_or_questions: TR/ES/GDR are technically composite formulas (each AND/depends on MB|RE|TA or GG), but the prompt names them as primitives to classify; classified as roots since each has its OWN boolean variable (fTR_b/fES_b/fGDR_b) AND is referenced by name within the locked FAUNA expression. Confirm bible convention.

MB/RE/TA are defined TWICE in the file: once at L99-L106 (bull_*/bear_* — used by F2/E3/FC) and again at L717-L737 (fMB_*/fRE_*/fTA_* — used by FAUNA). The arithmetic is identical. Treated as one root per (name, direction); the FAUNA-scoped definition is the canonical lock for the bible.
F2/E3/FC are first-defined-here LCD primitives — no other indicator owns them in the repo (PBJ/PB/PUP/PPD live in hvd-pbj-ppd, GZ/HV in hv-fvg-gz1-og). They are listed as ultra-combo-namespace roots.

ROC and LBWT are decomposed into 5 atoms each (b1..b5_roc, r1..r5_roc) — the 'primitive' name 'ROC' and 'LBWT' map to representative crossover/crossunder atoms (b1_roc/r1_roc for ROC, ld_xup/ld_xdn for LBWT). Other atoms (b2..b5_roc, r2..r5_roc) are listed under internal_helpers because they're never referenced standalone outside of bull2_cond/bear2_cond.

The branch tag was given as claude/add-txt-indicator-format-b4FUu in the prompt, but the working tree is currently on claude/organize-indicators-hierarchy-8JDw1. Recorded the prompt's value verbatim per its instruction.

anyBullRVOL/anyBearRVOL (L750-L751) deliberately differ from anyBullHW/anyBearHW (L870-L871) — the latter additionally include sigROC* and the Super combos. Both are used as gates in different composites; documented as internal helpers.

DISP_BULL/DISP_BEAR are listed as composites because the displacement primitive (range > 5*stdev * prevDisp) AND the FVG primitive are bound together in the same boolean (sigDISPBull). They have no NAME outside ultra-combo so are not roots.

internal_helpers.name: f_vob
internal_helpers.signature: f_vob(int len1, array<level> up_lvl, array<level> lo_lvl, float atr_prox, float atr_adj, float sup_mult) -> [bool t3_buy, bool t3_sell, float bp, float sp]
internal_helpers.purpose: Core engine (preserved-exactly per file header). On EMA(len1)/EMA(len1+13) crossover -> push lower bull zone with accumulated volume from lowest swing; on crossunder -> push upper bear zone from highest swing. Dedupes near duplicates within atr_prox by overwriting prior slot with all-na level; invalidates on close-breach. Returns T3 buy/sell flags (single-zone + pool*sup_mult dominance + price-inside-zone) and total bull/bear pool volumes.

internal_helpers.name: f_count_overlaps / f_draw_zone / f_process_zones
internal_helpers.purpose: MutEx priority-line drawing. f_process_zones walks each tier from A (highest) down to F (lowest), counting how many already-drawn zones the current zone overlaps. Priority 0 (no overlap) draws full top+bottom+mid+fill; priority 1/2/3 degrade to single midline of varying style. This is the 'mutually exclusive' visual: highest-sensitivity tier wins full treatment.

The ladder-watch's zA..zF roots conceptually map onto this file's zA-bull..zF-bull roots, but the two indicators run independent state arrays (no live data sharing inside Pine).

File is explicitly designed to emit through six MCP channels (data_get_study_values disabled - plot budget consumed; data_get_pine_labels via f_emit_label; data_get_pine_tables via comprehensive emission table; pine_get_console via log.info; alert() via Bloomberg-format strings; data_get_pine_lines via MutEx zone lines). uncertain_or_questions.q: The CHANGELOG (/home/user/indicators/vob/CHANGELOG.md) has only one entry, for VOB_LADDER_WATCH_v1.pine on 2026-05-04. There is no CHANGELOG entry for this file (VOB_Asym_T3x6_MutEx_Claude_v8 dated 2026-05-02 in its filename). Treat that as 'changelog covers companion only' rather than missing data.

The MutEx priority engine in f_process_zones (1075-1086) is called with tiers in order A,B,C,D,E,F bull then A,B,C,D,E,F bear. Earlier-pushed tiers (A) get full visual; later (F) get degraded. This is the named 'mutually exclusive' contract - confirmed by file header comment 39-43. No ambiguity, but worth flagging that 'priority' here is by tier letter (A wins) not by zone freshness or volume.

f_vob's dedup-by-overwrite-with-na pattern (lines 602, 616) plus the arr.shift() size cap at 15 (lines 605-606, 619-620) could in theory evict an active zone if 15 mostly-na slots accumulate before a real zone is pushed. Inherited characteristic also flagged in vob-ladder-watch extract.

Each tier exposes a SINGLE bull and SINGLE bear zone-creation marker root (zA-bull, zA-bear, etc.) per the LCD rule - even though f_vob can push multiple zones per tier-array over time. The named primitive is the birth-event boolean (fire_zb_*/fire_zs_*), not the zone object itself. Down-stream composites that need zone-set semantics (pools, counts, gaps) are listed as composites depending on the root set.

The indicator name 'VOB Asym T3 x6' references 'asymmetric' but the asym_threshold input (line 140) is documented as RESERVED for future Tier 1 asymmetric signals; T3 uses super_mult only. So the 'asymmetric' in the name currently refers to the file's six-tier ladder topology rather than a separate asym signal.

cross_indicator_dependencies.notes: f_bull_vob (lines 59-85) is documented in the file header and CHANGELOG as mirroring VOB Asym T3's bull half (no T3-smoothing, no MutEx pruning, bull-only). No code import — duplicated logic. zF..zA roots here are conceptual cousins of VOB Asym T3's six bull tiers but evaluated independently with their own arrays.
Five alertcondition() entries (lines 169-178) plus one alert() call (line 191) expect TradingView 'Any alert() function call' wiring per CHANGELOG instructions.

uncertain_or_questions.q: f_top_active selects the HIGHEST mid-price among active zones, but the comment on line 95 says 'most recent floor'. Are these always the same? In a strictly ascending market the newest zone is also the highest, but if a higher older zone has not been invalidated (close has not breached its lower) the helper would return that older zone, not the newest. May affect ladder semantics in choppy markets.

f_bull_vob deduplicates by writing level.new(na, na, na, na, na) into slots (lines 81, 83) rather than removing them; f_top_active correctly skips na mids. But the size cap arr.shift() at line 85 may evict an active zone if the array grows past 15 with mostly-na slots — a known characteristic shared with VOB Asym T3's engine.

Stage 6 disambiguation: this file's zA..zF roots share NAMES with VOB Asym T3's bull tiers but are independent computations (different arrays, no T3 smoothing, no cross-tier MutEx). The vob-ladder-watch:: namespace prefix is the disambiguator.

roots.plain_english: FAUNA bull bar: an up-bar that satisfies at least one of MB (Momentum Bar: body ≥1.6×ATR14, body/range ≥0.70, vol ≥1.8×AvgVol), RE (Range Expansion: range ≥2.2×ATR14, close near high, vol ≥1.8×AvgVol), or TA (Trend Acceleration: rising 50-SMA, Δclose ≥1.6×AvgΔ, vol ≥1.8×AvgVol); the GG (Gap-and-Go) family is only allowed when 2+ core-FAUNA hit AND body/range ≥0.80; excluded by TR/ES/GDR (post-weak/strong/gap-down-reversal pre-bar context).
roots.boolean_summary: conf AND (f_MB_b OR f_RE_b OR f_TA_b) AND NOT (f_TR_b OR f_ES_b OR f_GDR_b OR (f_GG_b AND NOT (core>=2 AND bodyRat>=0.80)))

roots.plain_english: FAUNA bear bar: mirror of FAUNA_BULL — down-bar satisfying at least one of MB/RE/TA bear families, with GG-bear only allowed via the 2-core + bodyRat≥0.80 path; excluded by mirror TR/ES/GDR.
roots.boolean_summary: conf AND (f_MB_r OR f_RE_r OR f_TA_r) AND NOT (f_TR_r OR f_ES_r OR f_GDR_r OR (f_GG_r AND NOT (core>=2 AND bodyRat>=0.80)))
